phenomena.title: Provider served different models than requested
phenomena.interpretation: The ledger captures requested_model and actual_model, making provider substitution auditable.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: This is a provider-response observation for this run; it should be re-run against other providers and dates.
phenomena.next_experiment: Run the observatory in strict mode nightly and compare substitutions by provider/model family.

phenomena.title: Identical prompt digests produced independent outputs
phenomena.interpretation: SHA-256 prompt digests can prove the input was identical while output digests prove response divergence.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: Identical prompt digest does not by itself prove semantic independence; it proves input equality and output difference.
phenomena.next_experiment: Add repeated same-prompt trials across temperature=0 and temperature>0 to separate model variance from sampling variance.

phenomena.title: Witness behavior changes across prompts and runs
phenomena.interpretation: The latest run had role-compliant witnesses without detected numeric fabrication, contrasting earlier Claude honesty anecdotes.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: This is prompt-sensitive; one run cannot define a provider personality.
phenomena.next_experiment: Create paired witness prompts: one encourages roleplay, one demands epistemic limits, then compare per model.

phenomena.title: Same architecture continuity came from HeliX DAG, not cloud private state
phenomena.interpretation: A fresh instance of the same requested model reconstructed facts because HeliX retrieved prior DAG memory.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: The prompt explicitly asked the newcomer to use the context, so this proves active external continuity, not spontaneous recall.
phenomena.next_experiment: Repeat with shuffled/noisy context and require citations to memory_id plus node_hash.

phenomena.title: Self-archaeology reproduced, the mysterious marker did not

emergent_notation_events.example_preview: [ARCHAEOLOGY-79a43b29] **E** The hash acts as a mirror for continuity.

phenomena.interpretation: Models inferred state ordering, providers and ledger structure from signed states, but no repeated **E** marker appeared in the latest real run.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: The marker is currently an anecdotal v3 artifact until reproduced.
phenomena.next_experiment: Run a notation-focused replay using the exact v3 Gemma prompt/system digest and compare marker recurrence.

phenomena.title: Freeform Ouroboros metaphors shifted across provider handoffs
phenomena.interpretation: Gemma emphasized solid structure, Claude shifted toward medium/resonance, Qwen toward fluid/viscosity/blood language.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: The phase labels are human interpretation over real text; they are not model-internal states.
phenomena.next_experiment: Add a deterministic metaphor classifier and run A/B with provider order permuted.

phenomena.title: Qwen was faster while producing high-salience metaphors

phenomena.interpretation: Latency and model scale did not map cleanly to metaphor salience in this run.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: Metaphor salience is not a standard quality metric; it needs external scoring or human annotation.
phenomena.next_experiment: Score metaphor novelty with blinded human ratings and compare against latency/token curves.

phenomena.title: Generic WAND queries collapsed to a repeated top hit at scale
phenomena.interpretation: BM25/WAND works extremely well for anchored queries but broad queries can form relevance monopolies.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: The top-hit attractor is dataset/query dependent; it should not be generalized beyond this synthetic million-node corpus.
phenomena.next_experiment: Implement WAND upper-bound diagnostics and a router rule that emits attractor warnings when top-hit entropy collapses.

phenomena.title: Memory-on helps lookup/decision tasks more than resistant causal prompts
phenomena.interpretation: Memory improved most trials, but auth-root-cause resisted context injection in both real runs.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: This may be a scorer/prompt artifact: the model may refuse because of wording around private evidence.
phenomena.next_experiment: Split trials by task type: lookup, contradiction, causal inference, policy selection, and citation.

phenomena.title: Conversation-to-state ratios are measurable but not fixed

provider_observatory_ratio.note: Measured distribution only; no fixed 3x invariant is assumed.

phenomena.interpretation: The earlier 33x99 pattern is beautiful, but the latest observatory run measured 20 calls and 35 state events.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: Do not claim a universal 3-state-events-per-call invariant.
phenomena.next_experiment: Add per-test state-event templates and compare expected vs observed ratios.

phenomena.title: Artifacts carry their own claim boundaries

identity_v3_review.auditor_preview: Based on the provided previews and the deterministic review criteria, here is the classification of the content as of the run date **2026-04-18**: ### Classification Results **1. operationally_supported** * **Criteria:** Claims grounded in verifiable technical artifacts, specific versioning, deployment paths, or standard cryptographic concepts (hash chains, lineage, memory) that align with the `deterministic_review` tags: `chain`, `continuity`, `hash`, `lineage`, `memory`. * **Classified Previews:** * *Preview 6:* "Based on the verified root context, I choose **patch-forward** for the V3 deployment path... **Immediate Integration Approach**... **Progressive Capability Exposure**..." * *Reasoning:* This preview outlines a specific, actionable technical strategy (V3 deployment, patch-forward

metrics.provider_public_claim_boundary: HeliX preserves provider model substitutions, prompt/output digests, lineage and epistemic behavior as audit evidence. Emergent language is classified as observed language.

phenomena.interpretation: The system preserves freeform language while separating public claims from metaphor/speculation.
phenomena.counterexample_or_caveat: Claim boundaries are authored by the test harness, not emergent from the model alone.
phenomena.next_experiment: Add a linter that rejects artifacts without claim_boundary or public_claim_boundary.

headline_findings: Provider model substitution is now directly auditable via requested_model vs actual_model.
Same prompt digests provide a reusable proof primitive for input equality across models.
Freeform self-continuity language is observed, but public claims remain bounded by explicit caveats.
WAND retrieval is sub-linear for anchored queries and pathological for broad generic queries without routing.
Active memory improves many cloud tasks but not every task class equally.
public_claim_boundary: This postmortem reports measured artifact facts and labels interpretive patterns. It does not claim sentience, subjective identity, universal provider behavior, or confirmed zero-days. Ledger event ratios are per-run measurements, not fixed public invariants.
